summary: Theme Switching Test: PARTIAL SUCCESS. The Zustand selector fix IS working - React components now correctly update background colors when theme changes (div[7] changes from rgb(254,252,255) to rgb(26,21,32)). HOWEVER, the document.body/html useEffect fix is NOT executing - body.style.backgroundColor remains empty despite the useEffect code being present in _layout.tsx.

critical.issue: useEffect to set document.body.style.backgroundColor is NOT executing on web platform. Despite code being present at lines 27-37, body.style.backgroundColor and html.style.backgroundColor remain empty ('') after theme changes.

integration_issues.issue: document.body and document.documentElement background colors are not being set. The useEffect dependency [theme.colors.background.primary] may not be triggering re-execution properly.

issues: Gray wrapper (rgb(242,242,242)) at div index 5 still visible - comes from SafeAreaProvider or react-native-web defaults

test_results.zustand_selector_fix_working: PASS - Individual selectors causing proper re-renders
test_results.react_component_theme_update: PASS - div[7] background changes correctly (dark: rgb(26,21,32), light: rgb(254,252,255))
test_results.visual_change_in_appearance_section: PASS - Background of theme options area changes visually

dark_mode_divs.note: CORRECT dark theme color from View in ThemedLayout

debug_findings.useEffect_not_executing: The useEffect at _layout.tsx lines 27-37 with Platform.OS === 'web' check is NOT setting document styles

fix_1_zustand_selectors.change: Changed from destructuring to individual selectors: useThemeStore((state) => state.themePreference)
fix_1_zustand_selectors.result: WORKING - Components re-render on theme change

fix_2_body_background_useEffect.change: Added useEffect to set document.body.style.backgroundColor on web
fix_2_body_background_useEffect.result: NOT WORKING - useEffect not executing or changes being overridden

action_items: CRITICAL: Debug why useEffect at _layout.tsx lines 27-37 is not setting document.body.style.backgroundColor
Add console.log inside the useEffect to verify it's being called with correct theme color
Check if something else is resetting document.body styles after the useEffect runs
Consider using CSS variables or inline styles on a global wrapper instead of document.body manipulation
Alternative: Add a global CSS file that sets body background based on a CSS class that React can toggle
critical_code_review_comments: The useEffect has correct dependency [theme.colors.background.primary] but may not be re-executing on theme changes
Platform.OS === 'web' check should be correct for Expo web builds
Consider adding a console.log to debug if the useEffect is firing at all

success_rate.frontend: 60% - Theme switching works at component level, but body background not set

context_for_next_testing_agent: Theme switching IS working at React component level - the View in ThemedLayout correctly updates its backgroundColor. The issue is the useEffect that sets document.body.style.backgroundColor is NOT executing or not persisting. Verify by adding console.log to the useEffect to see if it fires on theme changes. The gray wrapper (rgb(242,242,242)) at div[5] is separate issue from SafeAreaProvider.

comparison_with_previous_iteration.iteration_156_status: Theme not switching visually at all
comparison_with_previous_iteration.iteration_157_status: Theme switching WORKING at React level, but document.body not being set
comparison_with_previous_iteration.improvement: SIGNIFICANT - Zustand selector fix is working, components re-render correctly

recommended_debug_steps: 1. Add console.log('Setting body background to:', theme.colors.background.primary) inside the useEffect
2. Check if useEffect fires on initial mount and on theme changes
3. If useEffect fires but styles reset, check for competing CSS or other useEffects
4. Consider moving the document.body manipulation to the ThemeProvider itself
